name: statute-stack

escalateTo: split across slides (auto with autosplit: on)
capacity.note: past four jurisdictions the three-column rail collapses

split.note: Overflowing stack splits as an accent cover → its own native cards paginated (never flattened). lib/core/carousel.js cover-paginate.

description: Citation hierarchy — federal / state / local rows with citation, headline obligation, and status.
purpose: Use when three or four parallel jurisdictions need to read at a glance: each row carries the jurisdiction label, the citation, the obligation summary, and an effective-date marker.

heading.description: Slide heading framing what the three rows compare.

rows.description: One li per jurisdiction. Lead with the jurisdiction label as a plain text first line; nested ul items carry the citation (inline code), obligation summary, and status (inline code).

skeleton:
<!-- _class: statute-stack -->

## Jurisdiction comparison framing the three obligations.

- Federal `Citation`
  - Headline obligation in one sentence.
  - `Status or effective date`
- State `Citation`
  - Headline obligation in one sentence.
  - `Status or effective date`
- Local `Citation`
  - Headline obligation in one sentence.
  - `Status or effective date`
sample:
<!-- _class: statute-stack -->

## Children's data — three jurisdictions, three obligations.

- Federal `15 U.S.C. §6501`
  - Verifiable parental consent for under-13 personal data.
  - Operators must post a clear notice and a deletion route.
  - `In effect since 2000`
- State `Cal. Civ. §1798.120`
  - Opt-in for selling or sharing under-16 data; opt-out for over-16.
  - DSAR handling within 45 days; deletion verified.
  - `Enforced 2023`
- Local `NYC §22-1201`
  - Bias-audit obligation for AEDTs used in employment decisions.
  - Annual audit + candidate notice + public summary.
  - `Effective 2023`

whenToUse.title: Three parallel jurisdictions
whenToUse.body: Federal / state / local — or any three peer regimes — that the room must hold side-by-side. The hue rotation cues which row is which without a legend.

whenToUse.title: Citation plus obligation plus status
whenToUse.body: Each card is a three-part record: the citation pill (top-right of the header), the headline obligation in prose, and the status pill (bottom-left). Use when all three matter; for citation-only, reach for list-tabular.

whenToUse.title: Compliance briefings
whenToUse.body: Reach for statute-stack when the deck reads as a regulatory memo — counsel and operations need the same view of the same record at the same time.

antiPatterns.title: More than four rows
antiPatterns.body: The three-column rail collapses past four jurisdictions. For longer registers move to `lane` (table form) or split across two statute-stack slides by topic.

antiPatterns.title: Citation without obligation
antiPatterns.body: Without the headline obligation sentence, the layout reads as a citation list. Use list-tabular spec when only the citation matters.

antiPatterns.title: Mixed entry shapes
antiPatterns.body: Every row needs the same three parts — citation, obligation, status. A row missing the status pill or with prose instead of a citation breaks the visual contract.

related.name: list-tabular
related.when: the rows are citation-only references, no obligation prose

related.name: obligation-matrix
related.when: obligations cross-tab against actors or controls

related.name: authority-chain
related.when: the rows are a delegation lineage, not parallel jurisdictions

related.name: compare-table
related.when: the comparison is across criteria, not jurisdictions

hierarchy.label: Authority pyramid
hierarchy.caption: Stacks the rows vertically with descending row heights — Federal tallest, State mid, Local shortest. Use when the jurisdictions are nested in authority, not peers.
hierarchy.sample:
<!-- _class: statute-stack hierarchy -->

## Children's data — authority cascades downward.

- Federal `15 U.S.C. §6501`
  - Verifiable parental consent for under-13 personal data.
  - `In effect since 2000`
- State `Cal. Civ. §1798.120`
  - Opt-in for selling or sharing under-16 data; opt-out for over-16.
  - `Enforced 2023`
- Local `NYC §22-1201`
  - Bias-audit obligation for AEDTs used in employment decisions.
  - `Effective 2023`

bands.label: Horizontal bands
bands.caption: Each jurisdiction is a full-width card stacked at equal height — an at-a-glance scorecard. Same card anatomy as the default (citation top-right, status bottom-left); only the arrangement differs.
bands.sample:
<!-- _class: statute-stack bands -->

## Children's data — at-a-glance scorecard.

- Federal `15 U.S.C. §6501`
  - Verifiable parental consent for under-13 personal data.
  - `In effect since 2000`
- State `Cal. Civ. §1798.120`
  - Opt-in for selling or sharing under-16 data; opt-out for over-16.
  - `Enforced 2023`
- Local `NYC §22-1201`
  - Bias-audit obligation for AEDTs used in employment decisions.
  - `Effective 2023`

preemption.label: Preemption cascade
preemption.caption: Vertical stack with a downward arrow between each card. Use when the slide must show that the upper jurisdiction preempts or supersedes the one below.
preemption.sample:
<!-- _class: statute-stack preemption -->

## Federal preemption — how the cascade flows.

- Federal `15 U.S.C. §6501`
  - Sets the floor for under-13 personal data collection.
  - `Preempts state rules`
- State `Cal. Civ. §1798.120`
  - Stricter opt-in regime on top of COPPA's baseline.
  - `Survives preemption`
- Local `NYC §22-1201`
  - Bias-audit obligation distinct from privacy preemption scope.
  - `Independent of preemption`

lane.label: Markdown table
lane.caption: Pivots to a markdown table — columns for jurisdiction, citation, obligation, status. Use for register-style briefings where the audience scans down each column.
lane.sample:
<!-- _class: statute-stack lane -->

## Children's data — register view.

| Jurisdiction | Citation              | Headline obligation       | Status      |
| ------------ | --------------------- | ------------------------- | ----------- |
| Federal      | 15 U.S.C. §6501       | Parental consent <13 data | In effect   |
| State        | Cal. Civ. Code §1798  | Notice + opt-out + DSAR   | Enforced    |
| Local        | NYC §22-1201          | Annual AEDT bias audit    | Effective   |
